 FUN FACTS ABOUT CHOLE: She is the oldest cow in our herd. She is part Brown Swiss and part Guernsey which gives her her red color. Her udder is so big it hangs to the floor.

 FUN FACTS ABOUT CLEOPATRA: We call her Cleo for short. She looks like she has the eye makeup of Cleopatra (hence the name!) She is a sweet girl. Her mom is Hazel.

 Personality: Calm & Easygoing 
 FUN FACTS ABOUT MS. MCGREGOR: She is TINY compared to all our other cows. She is the first Jersey we have owned since moving to Texas. She loves 70’s rock. And she is the best mama cow we’ve ever seen.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2022
[TITLE]: A Cannulated Cow Lives With a Hole in Its Stomach | Amusing Planet
[METADATA/INTRO]: Imagine being able to look inside a cow. Call it curiosity, or the craving to reveal all of the world’s truths, but the cannulated cow is man’s experiment to discover what goes on in the digestive systems of a bovine,...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: A cannulated cow. Photo: Modern Farmer 
 What is a Cannulated Cow? 
 Within a cow live millions of independent microorganisms that work day and night to fuel its digestive system. Of its four stomachs, a cow’s rumen is where this magic happens—cellulose from plants is converted into energy in the 20-gallon pit. The symbiotic relationship allows the cow to thrive despite eating whatever it does, and lets the microbes exist in exchange for their positive effect on the mammal’s gut health. Now imagine if a cow got sick and lost the homeostasis that allowed good bacteria to nurse it back to health.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: Unknown Date
[TITLE]: Cow - Jukani Wildlife Sanctuary, Plettenberg Bay, South Africa
[METADATA/INTRO]: Cows form strong social bonds and often have best friends. Research has shown that cows become less stressed when they are with their preferred companions and can become anxious when separated from them. Cows have excellent memories and are capable...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Cows possess more cognitive abilities than they are often given credit for. They can solve problems, figure out mazes, and remember the solutions to challenges for extended periods. Some studies suggest that cows experience excitement when they solve a puzzle or learn a new task. 
 A cow’s hooves are made from keratin, the same protein that forms human fingernails and hair. The hooves grow continuously and must wear down naturally or be trimmed by a farmer to prevent overgrowth and hoof problems. 
 Cows form close relationships with their herd members, and when one dies or is removed, the others can show signs of grief, such as restlessness, lowing, and behavioral changes.
